hero.tagline: Home cooking, in the heart of Palma

sobre.label: Our story
sobre.p1: Casa Julio was born over thirty years ago by Julio and Carmen, a Mallorcan couple with the simple dream of cooking well and making people feel at home. What started as a family dining room in the Santa Catalina neighbourhood has become one of Palma's most beloved spots.
sobre.p2: We don't invent anything new here: we respect the produce, honour the seasons, and revive the recipes our mothers knew by heart. The rice dishes are cooked over a slow flame, the bread arrives fresh every morning from the bakery across the street, and the wine comes from wineries we know by name.
sobre.p3: Come and visit us and discover why our guests keep coming back. We'll welcome you with the same enthusiasm as on the very first day.

cartaPreview.label: What we cook
cartaPreview.subtitulo: Local produce, timeless recipes. Our menu changes with the seasons.
cartaPreview.cat0_desc: Pa amb oli, homemade croquettes, seasonal salads and tapas with local produce.
cartaPreview.cat1_desc: Mallorcan arroz brut, seafood paella and slow-cooked creamy rice with cuttlefish.
cartaPreview.cat2_desc: Salt-crusted sea bass, roast suckling pig, lamb chops on the grill and the day's fresh catch.
cartaPreview.cat3_desc: Filled ensaïmada, homemade egg custard, artisan almond ice cream from Mallorca.
